sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== Key objects ===")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ('M', 'p', 'G', 'W'):
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if node['pixels'] >= 5 or node['color'] == 'M':
            print(f"  color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, center~({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2},{(min(cols)+max(cols))//2})")

print("\n=== Bottom area ===")
for r in range(52, 63):
    line = ascii_lines[r]
    non_b = [(c, line[c]) for c in range(24, 32) if line[c] not in ('B',)]
    if non_b:
        print(f"Row {r}: {non_b}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Wait, the dark gray square now has only 8 pixels instead of 12. Let me check: originally it had 12 pixels (a diamond shape), now it has 8. The center pixel at (59,27) is now 'p' instead of 'G'. So the purple square is overlapping and replacing the center of the dark gray square.
It seems like clicking the magenta center when the purple square overlaps the dark gray square doesn't advance it further. Maybe I need to click the dark gray square's remaining pixels or the purple pixel at (59,27)?
Actually, let me reconsider. The dark gray square had 12 pixels originally (a diamond shape). Now it has 8 pixels, meaning 4 pixels were replaced. The center (59,27) is now purple. 
Maybe the goal is to completely convert the dark gray square to purple? Or maybe I need to click on the purple pixel at (59,27) which is now part of the dark gray square's position?
Let me try clicking (59,27) - the purple pixel that's now in the dark gray square's position.
